title: Election Day Turnout for Virginia Redistricting Measure Shows Significant Decline
summary: In-person turnout on Tuesday is trailing far behind that of last year’s governor’s race, which set a state record in a nonpresidential election.

title: Virginia’s Vote Is Crucial in the Fight for Control of Congress
summary: Also, Trump extends the cease-fire with Iran. Here’s the latest at the end of Tuesday.

title: Trust Trump? Iran’s Doubts Shadow Peace Talks.
summary: Iranian leaders fear being burned again by President Trump, who tore up a nuclear agreement reached during the Obama administration after lengthy negotiations.

title: U.S. Turns Up Pressure on Iraq to Distance Itself From Iran
summary: Washington is demanding that the Baghdad government dismantle Iran-backed Iraqi militias that have been attacking Americans and U.S. sites there recently.

title: Justice Dept. Charges Prominent Civil Rights Group With Financial Crimes
summary: Republicans have accused the Southern Poverty Law Center, which is best known for investigating hate groups, of unfairly targeting conservative and Christian organizations.

title: U.S. Abruptly Rescinds Subpoenas in John Brennan Inquiry
summary: The reversal came after the Justice Department replaced a career prosecutor with a loyalist to President Trump in the administration’s effort to charge the former C.I.A. chief with a crime.

title: Trump Is Said to Be in Talks to Send Afghans Who Aided U.S. Forces to Congo
summary: A U.S. aid worker said that the Afghans, who were evacuated to Qatar, would face a choice between moving to the Democratic Republic of Congo and living under the Taliban.

title: Tucker Carlson Says He Is ‘Tormented’ by His Past Support for Trump
summary: “I want to say I’m sorry for misleading people,” said the conservative commentator, who has broken sharply with the president over the war with Iran.

title: RFK Jr. Refused to Commit to Backing New CDC Director on Vaccines
summary: In a tense congressional hearing, the health secretary also said he bore no responsibility for the measles outbreak in the United States.

title: D.H.S. Will Run Out of Money for Paychecks in May, Secretary Says
summary: The issue threatens to renew chaos at airports as lawmakers remain divided over a deal to end the two-month shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security.

title: Could ‘A River Runs Through It’ Have Been a Hit Today?
summary: The autobiographical novella, first published 50 years ago, arguably created a new type of guy: the literary fly fisherman.

title: A Year After U.S.A.I.D.’s Death, Fired Workers Find Few Jobs and Much Loss
summary: People have plowed through savings, cashed out retirement funds and moved in with relatives. Former U.S.A.I.D. workers estimate that less than half have found full-time work.

title: Proposed Lifetime Smoking Ban to Become Law in Britain
summary: The proposal, which was approved by Parliament on Tuesday, will ban the supply or sale of tobacco products to anyone born in 2009 or after, permanently.

title: Imprisoned for 30 Years, He Wooed Her With Love Poems
summary: After a forced confession and a death sentence, a Kurdish poet spent 30 years in jail, where he discovered his voice. His literary champion, to everyone’s surprise, became his life partner.

title: Federal Appeals Court Upholds Texas Ten Commandments Law
summary: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit said the law does not violate the Constitution. The plaintiffs said they planned to ask the Supreme Court to reverse the decision.

title: Is a ‘Curse of the Mambino’ Plaguing the Mets? Mamdani, Say It Ain’t So.
summary: On April 9, Zohran Mamdani posed for a picture with the New York Mets mascots. Since the joyous photo op, the Mets have not won a game.

title: SpaceX Strikes Deal With Cursor for $60 Billion
summary: The potential acquisition comes as Elon Musk’s rocket and satellite maker, which has been emphasizing artificial intelligence, is preparing to go public.

title: A.I. Is Eliminating Jobs on Wall Street
summary: “A.I. gives us places to go we haven’t gone,” said one bank’s chief executive.

title: A.I. ‘Hallucinations’ Created Errors in Court Filing, Top Law Firm Says
summary: Sullivan & Cromwell apologized for submitting a court document that had fake citations created by artificial intelligence.

title: How Tim Cook’s Tech Uniform Helped Make Apple Trillions
summary: The unassuming look of the outgoing Apple chief executive was an asset, as he navigated pop culture, the president and following Steve Jobs.

title: How to Save Academia
summary: A Yale report offers some honest self-reflection on where the university went wrong.

title: How Israel Lost Its Way and How Trump Can Save Lebanon
summary: The smashing of a statue of Jesus by an Israeli soldier and the founding of four provocative settlements together show Israel’s thoughtless path forward.

title: Teotihuacán Pyramids Shooting: Gunman Carried Notes on U.S. Mass Shootings
summary: The man who killed one tourist at a Mexican pyramid and wounded several others had materials in his backpack tied to a 1999 U.S. attack, a possible reference to the Columbine High School massacre.

title: Sightings of Meteors Surge, and Scientists Aren’t Sure Why
summary: The frequency of fireballs in our planet’s skies seemed to grow in recent months. NASA and other meteor experts can’t agree on what explains it.

title: Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick, a Florida Democrat, Resigns From Congress
summary: Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick stands accused of stealing $5 million in federal funds. She said she would step down minutes before an ethics panel was to vote on whether to recommend expelling her.

title: These Salmon Got High on Cocaine. That Wasn’t the Craziest Part.
summary: Scientists in Sweden made an unexpected discovery when they exposed the fish to the illegal drug as well as another substance.
